headline: Broken Pathways
subtext: The cracked footpaths all over the Northern Beaches pose a considerable risk to pedestrians and cyclists alike. Ratepayers are increasingly frustrated at the Council's apparent inability to maintain these essential public amenities.
body:
Crackpot Councillors across the Northern Beaches are up in arms over the alarming number of cracked footpaths throughout the region - an issue that is causing significant concern among local ratepayers. It seems that every other week, another beloved pathway gives way underfoot, leaving residents to navigate around the hazardous debris.

Worried Council Chambers meetings have been taking place as councillors scramble for solutions. Some are proposing a 'bendy ladder' approach, where ladders materialise out of thin air whenever a path cracks - allowing pedestrians to climb over the damage unscathed.

The Steyne has become a particular point of contention, with many claiming that it is now almost impossible to cross without serious injury. "I used to be able to walk down The Corso and not have my bones rattled by uneven paving," says one frustrated local ratepayer.

In an attempt to address the issue, The Remy Digest has decided to launch its very own 'Fix a Path' campaign - encouraging readers to report any dangerous pathways in their neighbourhood. So, if you find yourself dodging broken footpaths like a superhuman ninja, please get in touch and help us out.

Meanwhile, back at Council Chambers, the debate rages on. Should we be using the term "rubbish" when referring to this situation - or should we stick with our traditional British/Australian roots and use "garbage"? It's enough to make anyone lose their colour.

As for the B-Line bus route? Well, it remains unscathed for now. But with these hazardous pathways on everyone's minds, perhaps it is only a matter of time before even public transport is forced off its route. We can only hope that our Council Cracks - I mean councillors - come up with an efficient solution soon.

For the record: if you're wondering why we haven't used the word "sidewalk", it's because this article is strictly adhering to the quintessential Australian style guide, and it would be highly remiss of us not to use "footpath" instead.

professor.title: From The Archives
professor.content: The archives are currently closed due to a flea infestation.

classifieds: For Sale: Broken AI. Contact Admin.

mystic.prediction: This Pisces: Parking at North Head isn't looking good for your day out, but don't fret - it's just a temporary real estate inconvenience on the Northern Beaches, and Bin Chickens won't be as scary once you know their coffee preferences.
